indications: Acute pain, fever, dysmenorrhoea, osteoarthritis
dosing_india: 100 mg twice daily with food; max 200 mg/day; max 15 days
contraindications: Hepatic impairment, active peptic ulcer, children <12 years, pregnancy, renal failure
warnings: Hepatotoxicity risk; not recommended for >15 days; banned in EU and withdrawn in several countries due to liver injury
adverse_reactions: Hepatotoxicity, GI upset, dizziness, hypersensitivity reactions
notes: Not FDA-approved; withdrawn from EU market due to hepatotoxicity; widely used in India

indications: Nausea, vomiting, gastroparesis, GERD, functional dyspepsia
dosing_india: 10 mg 3 times daily before meals; max 30 mg/day; short-term use recommended
contraindications: Prolactin-dependent tumors, GI haemorrhage or mechanical obstruction, QT prolongation, hepatic impairment
warnings: Cardiac risk (QT prolongation, sudden death at higher doses); restricted to short-term use
adverse_reactions: Galactorrhoea, QT prolongation, dry mouth, headache, extrapyramidal effects (rare)
notes: Not FDA-approved for systemic use in US; available OTC in India; EMA restricted to 10 mg max 3x/day for max 1 week

indications: Bronchial asthma, COPD, chronic bronchitis
dosing_india: 400 mg twice daily or 200 mg three times daily; SR preparation 400 mg once daily
contraindications: Hypersensitivity to xanthines, acute MI, seizures, peptic ulcer
warnings: Fewer cardiovascular effects than theophylline; less GI adverse effects; monitor in hepatic impairment
adverse_reactions: Nausea, palpitations, headache, insomnia; fewer than theophylline
pharmacokinetics: Well absorbed orally; T1/2 ~7 hours; hepatic metabolism; less potent adenosine antagonism than theophylline
notes: Not FDA-approved; used in India and parts of Europe as safer alternative to theophylline

indications: Type 2 diabetes mellitus inadequately controlled by metformin or glipizide alone
dosing_india: Metformin 500 mg + Glipizide 5 mg once or twice daily with meals; dose titrate as needed
contraindications: Renal impairment (eGFR <30), hepatic failure, contrast media use, pregnancy
warnings: Risk of hypoglycaemia (glipizide component); lactic acidosis risk (metformin component, rare); hold before contrast procedures
adverse_reactions: Hypoglycaemia, GI upset, lactic acidosis (rare), weight gain
notes: Fixed-dose combination widely used in India; separate components individually FDA-approved but this exact FDC not FDA-approved

indications: Type 2 diabetes mellitus
dosing_india: Metformin 500 mg + Gliclazide 80 mg once or twice daily with meals
contraindications: Renal impairment (eGFR <30), hepatic failure, pregnancy, type 1 DM
warnings: Hypoglycaemia risk; monitor renal function; hold before IV contrast
adverse_reactions: Hypoglycaemia, GI intolerance, lactic acidosis (rare), weight gain
notes: Gliclazide is widely used internationally; combination not as FDC in US/FDA

indications: Type 2 diabetes mellitus
dosing_india: Metformin 500 mg + Voglibose 0.2 mg twice or thrice daily with meals
contraindications: Renal impairment, hepatic disease, IBD, gastroparesis
adverse_reactions: Flatulence, bloating, diarrhoea, GI discomfort, lactic acidosis (rare)
notes: Voglibose not FDA-approved; widely used in India, Japan, Korea

drug_class: Proton pump inhibitor + Dopamine antagonist combination
indications: GERD, functional dyspepsia, peptic ulcer with nausea/gastroparesis symptoms
dosing_india: Pantoprazole 40 mg + Domperidone 30 mg SR once daily before breakfast
contraindications: QT prolongation, hepatic impairment, hypersensitivity
warnings: Cardiac risk from domperidone (QT prolongation); avoid in patients with cardiac disease
adverse_reactions: Headache, diarrhoea, nausea, QT prolongation, galactorrhoea
notes: Popular FDC in India; not approved as a combination in EU or USA

indications: Osteoarthritis, rheumatoid arthritis, ankylosing spondylitis, acute pain
dosing_india: 100 mg twice daily with food; max 200 mg/day
contraindications: Peptic ulcer, severe hepatic/renal impairment, CABG perioperative, pregnancy (3rd trimester)
warnings: GI bleeding, cardiovascular risk, renal impairment
adverse_reactions: GI upset, peptic ulcer, dizziness, elevated LFTs, peripheral oedema
pharmacokinetics: Prodrug converted to diclofenac in vivo; T1/2 ~4 hours; highly protein bound
notes: Metabolised to diclofenac in vivo; not FDA-approved; widely used in India, UK, and Europe

indications: Moderate to severe pain, post-operative pain, musculoskeletal pain
dosing_india: Diclofenac 50 mg + Paracetamol 500 mg twice or thrice daily
contraindications: Severe hepatic/renal disease, peptic ulcer, pregnancy, hypersensitivity
adverse_reactions: GI upset, hepatotoxicity, nephrotoxicity, cardiovascular risk
notes: Common OTC/Rx combination in India; not an FDC approved by FDA

drug_class: Penicillin + Beta-lactamase inhibitor + Uricosuric agent combination
indications: Susceptible bacterial infections where prolonged amoxicillin levels required
dosing_india: Amoxicillin 500 mg + Clavulanate 125 mg + Probenecid 500 mg twice daily
contraindications: Penicillin allergy, renal calculi, hyperuricaemia treatment already ongoing
adverse_reactions: Diarrhoea, rash, GI upset
notes: Probenecid delays renal excretion of amoxicillin, raising blood levels; niche combination used in India

drug_class: Short-acting beta-2 agonist (SABA) - R-enantiomer of salbutamol
indications: Bronchial asthma, COPD, bronchospasm
dosing_india: Inhaler: 45-90 mcg (1-2 puffs) every 4-6 hours as needed; Nebulisation: 0.31-1.25 mg
contraindications: Hypersensitivity, severe cardiovascular disease
warnings: Fewer cardiac side effects than racemic salbutamol; tachycardia, hypokalaemia possible
adverse_reactions: Tremor, tachycardia, hypokalaemia, headache, palpitations
pharmacokinetics: Active R-enantiomer of salbutamol; T1/2 ~3.3 hours; rapid onset 5 min
notes: Available in India; FDA approved as levalbuterol in US (same molecule, different spelling)

indications: Hyperthyroidism, Graves disease, toxic nodular goitre, preparation for thyroid surgery
dosing_india: Initial: 15-40 mg/day in divided doses; maintenance 5-15 mg/day
contraindications: Agranulocytosis, severe hepatic impairment, pregnancy (relative)
warnings: Agranulocytosis (potentially fatal); monitor WBC; crosses placenta — fetal hypothyroidism
adverse_reactions: Agranulocytosis, rash, urticaria, arthralgia, hepatitis, cholestasis
pharmacokinetics: Prodrug converted to methimazole in vivo; onset 12-24 hours
notes: Converted to methimazole (which is FDA-approved); carbimazole itself is used in UK, India, Europe but not directly in USA

indications: Post-operative and post-traumatic oedema, sinusitis, fibrocystic breast disease, carpal tunnel syndrome
dosing_india: 5-10 mg 3 times daily (enteric coated tablets to protect from gastric acid)
contraindications: Bleeding disorders, anticoagulant therapy, pregnancy
warnings: Limited high-quality evidence; not for use in haemophilia or with anticoagulants
adverse_reactions: GI upset, skin reactions, hypersensitivity, rare hepatotoxicity
notes: Widely used enzyme supplement in India; not FDA-approved as a pharmaceutical; evidence base is limited

indications: Post-operative and post-traumatic inflammation and oedema, soft tissue injuries
dosing_india: 2 tablets 3-4 times daily between meals; enteric coated
contraindications: Coagulation disorders, hepatic disease, pregnancy
adverse_reactions: GI disturbances, hypersensitivity, nausea
notes: Combination not FDA-approved; commonly prescribed in India for inflammation post-injury or surgery

indications: Moderate to severe pain, post-operative pain, neuropathic pain
dosing_india: Paracetamol 325 mg + Tramadol 37.5 mg: 1-2 tablets every 4-6 hours; max 8 tablets/day
contraindications: Seizure disorder, MAO inhibitor use within 14 days, hepatic impairment, respiratory depression
warnings: Abuse potential (tramadol); seizure risk; serotonin syndrome; dependence
adverse_reactions: Nausea, vomiting, dizziness, constipation, headache, seizures (rare)
notes: Also FDA-approved as Ultracet in USA; widely used in India

indications: Community-acquired pneumonia, acute bacterial sinusitis, skin and soft tissue infections, UTI
dosing_india: 200-300 mg twice or thrice daily for 5-7 days
contraindications: Hypersensitivity to beta-lactams, severe renal impairment
warnings: Cross-reactivity with penicillins possible; may cause seizures at high doses
adverse_reactions: Diarrhoea, nausea, rash, elevated liver enzymes
notes: Not FDA-approved; widely used in India and Japan as oral antibiotic for resistant infections

indications: Respiratory tract infections, UTI, skin infections, otitis media caused by beta-lactamase producing organisms
dosing_india: Cefpodoxime 200 mg + Clavulanate 125 mg twice daily for 5-10 days
contraindications: Cephalosporin allergy, severe renal impairment
adverse_reactions: Diarrhoea, nausea, rash, Clostridium difficile colitis
notes: Cefpodoxime alone is FDA-approved; this combination FDC is not FDA-approved but widely used in India

indications: Peripheral neuropathy, B12 deficiency, diabetic neuropathy, megaloblastic anaemia
dosing_india: 500 mcg 3 times daily orally; 500 mcg IM/IV injection 3 times weekly
contraindications: Polycythaemia vera (relative), Leber's optic neuropathy
adverse_reactions: Generally well tolerated; rare allergic reactions, acne, diarrhoea
notes: Active form of vitamin B12; widely preferred over cyanocobalamin in India and Japan for neuropathy; not separately FDA-approved (cyanocobalamin is the FDA standard)

indications: Diabetic peripheral neuropathy, oxidative stress-related conditions
dosing_india: 300-600 mg once daily; IV 300-600 mg in diabetic neuropathy
contraindications: Thiamine deficiency (relative), pregnancy
adverse_reactions: Nausea, vomiting, hypoglycaemia (additive with antidiabetics), skin rash
notes: Used as pharmaceutical in Germany/India for diabetic neuropathy; FDA-approved as dietary supplement in USA, not as prescription drug

drug_class: Anticonvulsant/Neuropathic pain agent + Vitamin B12 analogue combination
indications: Diabetic neuropathy, post-herpetic neuralgia, fibromyalgia with B12 deficiency
dosing_india: Pregabalin 75 mg + Methylcobalamin 750 mcg twice daily
contraindications: Hypersensitivity to pregabalin, severe renal impairment
warnings: CNS depression, dizziness, weight gain; pregabalin has abuse potential
adverse_reactions: Dizziness, somnolence, oedema, weight gain, blurred vision
notes: Popular FDC in India; pregabalin alone is FDA-approved; this combination not FDA-approved

drug_class: Proton pump inhibitor + Dopamine antagonist combination
indications: GERD, peptic ulcer with nausea, functional dyspepsia
dosing_india: Rabeprazole 20 mg + Domperidone 30 mg SR once daily before breakfast
contraindications: QT prolongation, hepatic impairment, domperidone hypersensitivity
warnings: Cardiac risk from domperidone; hypomagnesaemia with long-term PPI use
adverse_reactions: Headache, diarrhoea, QT prolongation, galactorrhoea
notes: PPI+domperidone FDC widely used in India; not FDA-approved as combination

indications: Hypertension, cardiovascular risk reduction
dosing_india: Telmisartan 40 mg + Amlodipine 5 mg once daily
contraindications: Pregnancy, bilateral renal artery stenosis, severe hepatic impairment
adverse_reactions: Peripheral oedema, dizziness, hyperkalaemia, cough (rare with ARB)
notes: Twynsta (same combination) is FDA-approved in USA; widely used in India under various brand names

drug_class: HMG-CoA reductase inhibitor + Fibric acid derivative combination
indications: Mixed dyslipidaemia, high cardiovascular risk with combined hyperlipidaemia
dosing_india: Rosuvastatin 10 mg + Fenofibrate 145 mg once daily with evening meal
contraindications: Active liver disease, pregnancy, severe renal impairment, myopathy history
warnings: Myopathy/rhabdomyolysis risk; monitor LFTs and CK; avoid grapefruit
adverse_reactions: Myalgia, elevated CK, hepatotoxicity, GI symptoms, headache
notes: Components individually FDA-approved; fixed combination widely used in India for mixed dyslipidaemia

drug_class: Leukotriene receptor antagonist + Second-generation antihistamine combination
indications: Allergic rhinitis, urticaria, bronchial asthma with allergic component
dosing_india: Montelukast 10 mg + Fexofenadine 120 mg once daily at night
contraindications: Hypersensitivity, severe hepatic impairment
warnings: Neuropsychiatric events with montelukast (depression, suicidal ideation - FDA black box); avoid alcohol with fexofenadine
adverse_reactions: Headache, dizziness, GI upset, neuropsychiatric effects (rare)
notes: Popular allergy combination in India; montelukast+levocetirizine is another common India FDC; components individually FDA-approved

drug_class: Leukotriene receptor antagonist + Second-generation antihistamine combination
indications: Allergic rhinitis, chronic urticaria, allergic asthma
dosing_india: Montelukast 10 mg + Levocetirizine 5 mg once daily at night
contraindications: Hypersensitivity, severe renal impairment (levocetirizine), neuropsychiatric disorders (montelukast)
warnings: Montelukast FDA black box warning for neuropsychiatric events; sedation possible
adverse_reactions: Somnolence, headache, dry mouth, neuropsychiatric events (rare)
notes: One of the most commonly prescribed combinations in India for allergic conditions; not an approved FDC in USA
